labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose
labs.actionRequired: Discontinue NSAID if >30% increase in creatinine
labs.clinicalContext: NSAIDs can cause nephrotoxicity and contribute to acute kidney injury, requiring monitoring when used with ACEi/ARB

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose
labs.clinicalContext: NSAIDs can interfere with kidney potassium excretion and contribute to hyperkalemia, especially when used with RAS blockers

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Particularly important to monitor in patients with CKD, diabetes, decompensated heart failure, volume depletion, advanced age, or those on RAS blockers
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: History of the use of over-the-counter nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), supplements, and herbal treatments should be pursued, and patients should be counseled to discontinue these remedies if present. Review for causes of AKI, correct volume depletion, reassess concomitant medications (e.g., diuretics, NSAIDs)
